notes: Default controller curve for address payloads.
Always available; no feature gates required.

checks: Reject payloads that are not exactly 32 bytes before decompressing the point.
Decompress using the Ed25519 canonical check and reject small-order/non-canonical points.
Require s < L when validating signatures and ensure clusters advertise curve id 0x01 in crypto.allowed_curve_ids.

notes: Reserved for ML-DSA rollout.
Available only in builds that enable the `ml-dsa` feature.

checks: Accept only 32-byte seeds generated by the selected ML-DSA profile (44/65/87) before expansion.
Derive the public key via pqcrypto_dilithium::<profile> and reject seeds that fail to decode.
Enforce pqcrypto_dilithium::<profile>::signature_bytes() for signatures and require the `ml-dsa` curve id to be listed in crypto.allowed_curve_ids.

entries.public_key_encoding: Placeholder identifier used only for capability negotiation; actual GOST controllers encode their TC26 coordinates using the parameter-specific entries below.
entries.signature_encoding: Uses the canonical 32-byte (r||s) or 64-byte (r||s) layout once a TC26 parameter set is selected.
notes: Do not emit production addresses with id 0x03; it exists solely so provisioning flows can advertise upcoming GOST readiness.
Switch to the parameterized identifiers (0x0A–0x0F) as soon as the corresponding TC26 set is enabled in crypto.allowed_curve_ids.

checks: Admission and SDKs must reject any controller payload that attempts to use id 0x03 because the placeholder carries no concrete domain parameters.
Provisioning/readiness probes MAY map id 0x03 to the TC26 registry to express intent, but controller payloads must move to the parameter-set specific identifiers before blockchain submission.

notes: Available for Nexus account controllers and multisig members.
Leverages deterministic ECDSA signing with low-s normalization; payloads must use SEC1-compressed public keys.

checks: Reject payloads that are not exactly 33 bytes before decompressing the SEC1 point.
Parse with SEC1 compressed rules and fail closed on invalid or non-canonical points.
Verify signatures using the canonical 64-byte r||s encoding produced by the RustCrypto k256 backend.

entries.public_key_encoding: TC26 param set A point (little-endian bytes)
notes: Reserved for GOST 256-bit param set A.
Requires the `gost` feature before use.

checks: Interpret the payload as little-endian (x || y) coordinates and reject values >= p or the identity point.
Ensure coordinates decompress against the TC26 param set A domain parameters.
Require canonical 32-byte r and s limbs during signature verification.

entries.public_key_encoding: TC26 param set B point (little-endian bytes)
notes: Reserved for GOST 256-bit param set B.
Requires the `gost` feature before use.

checks: Interpret the payload as little-endian (x || y) coordinates expressed over the TC26 param set B domain.
Reject points whose coordinates >= p or point-at-infinity encodings.
Require canonical 32-byte r and s limbs during signature verification.

entries.public_key_encoding: TC26 param set C point (little-endian bytes)
notes: Reserved for GOST 256-bit param set C.
Requires the `gost` feature before use.

checks: Interpret the payload as little-endian (x || y) coordinates expressed over the TC26 param set C domain.
Reject coordinates that are >= p or the identity point.
Require canonical 32-byte r and s limbs during signature verification.

entries.public_key_encoding: TC26 param set A 512-bit point (little-endian bytes)
notes: Reserved for GOST 512-bit param set A.
Requires the `gost` feature before use.

checks: Interpret the payload as little-endian (x || y) coordinates with 64-byte limbs over the TC26 512-bit param set A domain.
Reject coordinates >= p or identity encodings before attempting to decompress.
Require canonical 64-byte r and s limbs during signature verification.

entries.public_key_encoding: TC26 param set B 512-bit point (little-endian bytes)
notes: Reserved for GOST 512-bit param set B.
Requires the `gost` feature before use.

checks: Interpret the payload as little-endian (x || y) coordinates with 64-byte limbs over the TC26 512-bit param set B domain.
Reject coordinates >= p or identity encodings before attempting to decompress.
Require canonical 64-byte r and s limbs during signature verification.

entries.public_key_encoding: DistID length (u16 BE) + DistID bytes + SEC1 uncompressed SM2 point
notes: Reserved for the SM2 rollout.
Requires the `sm` feature before use.

checks: Decode the DistID length prefix (u16 BE), validate the DistID bytes, and ensure the remaining payload is 65 bytes before parsing the point.
Parse the SEC1 uncompressed point and reject invalid encodings before invoking the SM2 verifier.
Apply the configured DistID to the verification context and reject signatures that do not bind to it.
Require canonical 32-byte r and s limbs and ensure curve id 0x0F is present in crypto.allowed_curve_ids before admission.
